name: Fresh Vegetable Stuffing (from Chicago Tribune Magazine
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Spinach leaves, washed, stemmed and shredded
2 Cups Homemade Bread Crumbs (made from 3 or 4 slices of bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add carrots and sauté about 2 minutes until slightly softened.
2. Add onions, celery, thyme, tarragon, sage, basil, salt, chives, black pepper, zucchini, spinach, squash, and white wine to the skillet. Cook for about 4 minutes until vegetables are lightly soft.
3. Remove from heat and transfer contents to a large bowl. Stir in bread crumbs and parmesan cheese.
- If using as stuffing, chill in refrigerator for 2 hours before using.
- If baking separately, preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Butter a 3-quart casserole dish and place the mixture inside. Bake until warm.
This recipe is sufficient for stuffing one 14 lb turkey or two roasting chickens. Adjust quantities according to the size of poultry being used.
